name: Dar-us-Sakina
mission: DAR-US-SAKINA IS A 501(C)(3) NONPROFIT ORGANIZATION DEDICATED TO INCLUSION OF INDIVIDUALS WITH DISABILITIES IN THE MUSLIM COMMUNITY OF HOUSTON. OUR PLATFORM IS A SAFE SPACE FOR PEOPLE OF ALL ABILITIES AND THEIR CAREGIVERS. WE INSPIRE THE COMMUNITY TO ABANDON STIGMAS AND EMBRACE INDIVIDUALS WITH DISABILITIES WITH EMPATHY AND RESPECT. TOGETHER, WE ARE BETTER.
scoreSummary: Dar-us-Sakina shows good alignment and moderate impact, with zakat compliance (minor risk deductions applied).

evaluationSources: Grant for Volunteer Program Enhancement (Islamic Relief)
Grant for Data Process Refinement (United Way)

zakatClaimEvidence: Dar-us-Sakina offers its services to all special needs individuals and their caregivers within the Houston community, but reserves its Zakat fund for Muslim families who are in financial need or crisis. Zakat Services: For special needs families in crisis, we are committed to providing comprehensive support through our Zakat Fund, including funding of emergency respite care, counseling, necessary evaluations, advocacy and durable medical equipment. On their donation page, 'Zakat' is listed as a campaign option. (Source: https://www.dushouston.org/)

all_citations.claim: The charity has a Charity Navigator score of 93.0/100 and a program expense ratio of 72.2%.
Total revenue for the period was $1,115,693.
The mission is to provide inclusive opportunities for individuals with disabilities in the Houston Muslim community.
The organization focuses on empowering individuals through community-based programs.
Dar-us-Sakina operates programs including the Sakina Sunday School and Volunteer Enrichment Program.
The organization supports families through a Zakat Fund and provides inclusive educational support hours.
Dar-us-Sakina is zakat-eligible and provides various donation options for supporters.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a GMG score of 55/100, reflecting a solid financial foundation and high donor alignment, balanced by a need for more direct impact data.
areas_for_improvement: Limited third-party impact evaluations to verify long-term beneficiary outcomes
Moderate data confidence levels regarding specific program scale

dimension_explanations.alignment: With an alignment score of 31/50, the charity shows high donor fit for those seeking to support religious and cultural causes within the disability sector [3][6]. Its zakat-eligible status and mission to serve the Muslim community in Houston create a strong sense of urgency and track record for local donors [8][9].
dimension_explanations.impact: The impact score of 25/50 reflects a focus on capacity building through inclusive educational support hours and specialized enrollment [7][8]. While the organization maintains a healthy program expense ratio of 72.2%, the lack of specific cost-per-beneficiary data limits the overall impact rating [1][2].

baseline_narrative.headline: Dar-us-Sakina provides inclusive educational and social opportunities for individuals with disabilities within the Houston Muslim community.
strengths: High financial accountability with a Charity Navigator score of 93.0/100
Strong fundraising efficiency, spending only $0.06 to raise every $1
Robust liquidity with 15.9 months of working capital available
baseline_narrative.summary: Based in Houston, Dar-us-Sakina focuses on empowering individuals with special needs through specialized programs like the Sakina Sunday School and professional internships [3][7]. The organization is recognized as zakat-eligible, allowing it to support families through a dedicated Zakat Fund [8][9].

strengths.detail: The organization maintains an <cite id="5">overall Charity Navigator score of 93.0/100</cite>, reflecting high standards of transparency and governance.
Dar-us-Sakina demonstrates lean operations, spending only <cite id="8">$0.13 to raise every $1</cite> in total revenue.
The charity holds <cite id="9">15.9 months of working capital</cite>, providing significant financial stability for its specialized programs.

strengths_deep_dive: Dar-us-Sakina's financial health is a primary driver of its credibility. The organization achieved a <cite id="10">perfect 100 score in both Culture and Leadership</cite> beacons from Charity Navigator, indicating a healthy internal environment and strategic clarity. Their revenue model is increasingly sustainable, showing a <cite id="11">3-year revenue CAGR of 24.6%</cite>. This growth allows them to maintain <cite id="12">two active masjid sites</cite> for program delivery, ensuring that specialized disability services are integrated directly into the local Muslim community infrastructure.
rich_narrative.summary: Dar-us-Sakina is a Houston-based nonprofit dedicated to providing <cite id="1">inclusive educational and social opportunities</cite> for individuals with disabilities within the Muslim community. The organization operates a range of specialized services, including the <cite id="2">Sakina Sunday School and the Teen and Adult Program (TAP)</cite>, which focus on religious education and life skills. Despite its relatively recent growth, the charity has established a strong financial foundation, reporting <cite id="3">$540,354 in total revenue</cite> for the 2023 tax year. A significant portion of this growth is driven by community support, with <cite id="4">$398,320 coming from direct contributions</cite>. The organization maintains a high level of fiscal responsibility, evidenced by an <cite id="5">overall score of 93.0/100 from Charity Navigator</cite>. Furthermore, Dar-us-Sakina is <cite id="6">verified as zakat-eligible</cite>, specifically serving the asnaf categories of the poor (fuqara) and needy (masakin) through its dedicated Zakat Fund, which <cite id="7">supports families of students with special needs</cite>.
